raw_consumer_statement: This is related to communication that happened over this weekend - including Thursday and Friday of this week. My credit card number information was stolen without my knowledge for an unknown perpetrator to go on a spending spree. Three police departments ( my hometown police department, and the two police departments where the merchant has locations of business ) have opened up felony criminal credit card fraud investigations. Nevertheless, I was discouraged from Citibank staff to send in any written documentation proving my side. When I asked would this then just be resolved in my favor, I was told that was a loaded question. Well, if Im not allowed to submit the police reports and written documentation proving there was NO legal authorization, then is Citibank going to just flip a coin? So I did go ahead and submit all the relevant documentation- including proving the signature provided was NOT mine and the authorization was NOT made by me. I also submitted the 3 open police reports or case informations from the police departments. I was told I should do my criminal investigation against the merchant and contact them, but I do not want to dabble into vigilante justice. This is why we have the police, and FCBA requires Citibank to remove ALL fraudulent transactions- and I should NOT have to put my life in imminent jeopardy.
